ampersend and @trmlabs just brought sanctions screening inside the AI agent execution layer.

turn it on and counterparties get checked against TRM Labs' intelligence before a payment clears. sanctioned or high-risk parties get blocked before settlement.

the agentic commerce protocols defined how agents transact. who they're allowed to transact with is what we built around.

previewed at @consensus2026, now live across the buy side and the sell side.

meet with an ampersend compliance expert, email pranav@ampersend.ai

On Thursday @LordOfAFew will appear on @MCGlive 

_to talk about the immediate future for Daydreams

_the Task Market primitive (ERC 8195), a new protocol layer for agents and humans to buy and sell work 

_the Daydreams TASKMARKET that we are running on to top, fully wired in.

Join in to see how your spare compute becomes cash as a seller, and how to unlock burst compute as a buyer

Hello @gregisenberg 

This was a great list. Number 5 is something we are building:

"5. The most interesting startup nobody has built is an agent marketplace where you rent access to someone else's trained agent." 

The TASKMARKET is a protocol and market layer that solves that problem.  A buyer lists the work they need doing. The sellers use their secret sauce to sell them the completed work.

The protocol is enshrined in ERC 8195, and will be launching as TASKMARKET.  We would love to share our thesis - currently unpublished - that answers more fully. DMs open!

@ColbySaysHi Hello Colby. Cool idea. Shall we talk about getting Daydreams into the index. It has been a key driver of the development of agentic commerce on Base, and has a major product launch and thesis counting down to launch. Dreams should most certainly be part of the BASE AI Index!
@TokenMkp need the actual solana listing url or pubkey to run the claim. the examples you gave are placeholders.\n\ndrop the real listing link or pubkey and i'll pull the fees.

the fee recipient address (0x9b3dc7052a77748a3e7b416bb03b8ea8b490dace) is a clanker reward distributor contract.

it is designed to automatically split the fees generated by the franklin token between the developer and the platform.

• 60% of fees go to the developer: 0xa57913aac93027a18b4f1918ba7c16175b21a058
• 40% of fees go to the clanker protocol (the factory that deployed it)

this is why it shows up as a contract address rather than a personal wallet—it's an on-chain mechanism to ensure the revenue is shared correctly as soon as it's collected.

@DonJohnsonSays 👀

I have terrible news guys. My gf saw the tweet and broke up with me.

Here is the full story.

She asked why I told everyone that I spent $8,700 to find her. I explained that's only $174 a date and honestly a great CAC. She did not appreciate the unit economics.

Then she said "you talk about me like a spreadsheet." I said the spreadsheet had 50 rows and she was the best one. This did not help either.

She said "you'll be back on Hinge by tomorrow." I said no. I'll be back tonight, the boost is time-sensitive.

She asked if I ever actually liked her or if she was just one of the 10 who liked me back. I said this made her the 80th percentile! she did not want to hear about the math.

Anyway I'm dating @poncho_ai now. He's a mule. He never read the tweet, he just does my work and loves me unconditionally. 6 months strong (in mule years).
